---
title: "Relationship to legacy PolyLink and PolyLinkR"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Relationship to legacy PolyLink and PolyLinkR}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>")
```

## Context

Earlier **PolyLink** and **PolyLinkR** releases on GitHub introduced related file types and biological narratives around pathway enrichment and polygenic signals. This **polylinkR** line keeps the scientific idea—gene-set scores with linkage-aware nulls—whilst exposing a modular `plR_*` pipeline and modern packaging (tests, **pkgdown**, **renv**).

## What changed in the public API

Legacy entry points (for example monolithic driver functions) are superseded by `plR_read()`, `plR_permute()`, `plR_rescale()`, and `plR_prune()`. Review `NEWS.md` and GitHub pull requests when porting old scripts.

## References

- PolyLinkR repository: <https://github.com/ACAD-UofA/PolyLinkR>
- PolyLink repository: <https://github.com/ACAD-UofA/PolyLink>

This vignette is informative only; it does not change algorithmic defaults in the code.
